questions.text: Carbon forms bonds primarily by:
questions.explanation: Carbon has 4 valence electrons and forms covalent bonds by sharing them to achieve a stable octet configuration.

questions.text: The property of self-linking of carbon atoms to form long chains is called:
questions.explanation: Catenation is the unique ability of carbon to form stable bonds with other carbon atoms, resulting in long chains or rings.

questions.text: Hydrocarbons containing only single bonds between carbon atoms are called:
questions.explanation: Saturated hydrocarbons with single C-C bonds are known as alkanes.

questions.text: Which of the following is an unsaturated hydrocarbon?
questions.explanation: Ethene contains a double bond between carbon atoms, making it an unsaturated hydrocarbon.

questions.text: The general formula for Alkenes is:
questions.explanation: Alkenes are hydrocarbons with at least one double bond and follow the general formula CnH2n.

questions.text: Buckminsterfullerene is an allotrope of carbon containing how many atoms?
questions.explanation: C60 or Buckminsterfullerene is a spherical molecule consisting of 60 carbon atoms arranged in pentagons and hexagons.

questions.text: A functional group -CHO represents:
questions.explanation: -CHO is the functional group for the aldehyde family.

questions.text: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structures are called:
questions.explanation: Isomerism occurs when molecules have identical chemical formulas but distinct arrangements of atoms.

questions.text: Which gas is released during the complete combustion of hydrocarbons?
questions.explanation: Complete combustion of hydrocarbons in sufficient oxygen produces carbon dioxide and water.

questions.text: The process of converting unsaturated oils to saturated fats is called:
questions.explanation: Hydrogenation involves the addition of hydrogen to unsaturated bonds in the presence of a catalyst like Nickel.

questions.text: Which functional group is present in CH3COCH3 (Acetone)?
questions.explanation: Acetone contains the carbonyl group (>C=O) bonded to two other carbon atoms, which is a ketone.

questions.text: The successor of methane (CH4) in the homologous series is:
questions.explanation: In an alkane homologous series, members differ by a -CH2 unit. Methane + CH2 = Ethane.

questions.text: What is the catalyst used in the industrial hydrogenation of vegetable oils?
questions.explanation: Nickel or Palladium are typically used as catalysts for the addition of hydrogen to vegetable oils.

questions.text: Reaction of methane with chlorine in the presence of sunlight is a:
questions.explanation: One or more hydrogen atoms in methane are replaced by chlorine atoms, which is a substitution reaction.

questions.text: The common name for Ethanoic acid is:
questions.explanation: Ethanoic acid is widely known as acetic acid.

questions.text: A 5-8% solution of acetic acid in water is called:
questions.explanation: Vinegar is a dilute solution of acetic acid used in food preservation.

questions.text: Pure ethanoic acid is called 'glacial' acetic acid because:
questions.explanation: Its melting point is 290 K, causing it to freeze into ice-like crystals in winter.

questions.text: The reaction of an acid and an alcohol to form a sweet-smelling substance is:
questions.explanation: Esters are formed by the reaction of carboxylic acids and alcohols, typically in the presence of an acid catalyst.

questions.text: Esters react in the presence of an alkali to give back alcohol and sodium salt of acid. This is:
questions.explanation: This reaction is used in the preparation of soaps, hence called saponification.

questions.text: Soaps are ______ salts of long chain carboxylic acids.
questions.explanation: Soaps are specifically sodium or potassium salts of long-chain fatty acids.

questions.text: The 'tail' of a soap molecule is:
questions.explanation: The long hydrocarbon tail of soap is hydrophobic and dissolves in oils/grease.

questions.text: Soap forms 'scum' with hard water due to the presence of:
questions.explanation: Hard water contains Ca2+ and Mg2+ ions which react with soap to form an insoluble precipitate called scum.

questions.text: Which of the following works efficiently even in hard water?
questions.explanation: Detergents do not form insoluble precipitates with Calcium and Magnesium ions.

questions.text: The structure formed by soap molecules in water where tails point inward and heads point outward is:
questions.explanation: Micelles are clusters where hydrophobic tails trap the dirt/oil in the center.

questions.text: In the homologous series of alkanes, the difference in molecular mass between two adjacent members is:
questions.explanation: Successive members differ by -CH2 group (C=12, H=1+1, Total=14 u).

questions.text: What type of flame is produced by the combustion of saturated hydrocarbons in sufficient air?
questions.explanation: Saturated hydrocarbons generally burn with a clean blue flame due to complete combustion.

questions.text: Unsaturated hydrocarbons burn with a ______ flame.
questions.explanation: Because they have a high percentage of carbon, incomplete combustion occurs, leading to a yellow sooty flame.

questions.text: Which substance is used to oxidize ethanol to ethanoic acid?
questions.explanation: Alkaline KMnO4 or acidified K2Cr2O7 are strong oxidizing agents used in this conversion.

questions.text: Reaction of ethanol with sodium metal releases which gas?
questions.explanation: Alcohols react with sodium to form sodium ethoxide and hydrogen gas.

questions.text: What happens when ethanol is heated with concentrated sulphuric acid at 443 K?
options: It is dehydrated to form ethene
questions.explanation: Concentrated H2SO4 acts as a dehydrating agent, removing water from ethanol to produce ethene.

questions.text: Ethanoic acid reacts with sodium hydrogen carbonate to evolve:
questions.explanation: Like most acids, it reacts with carbonates and bicarbonates to produce CO2 gas with brisk effervescence.

questions.text: Diamond is a poor conductor of electricity because:
options: It has a low melting point
questions.explanation: In diamond, all four valence electrons of carbon are involved in strong covalent bonds, leaving no mobile electrons.

questions.text: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity because:
options: It has one free electron per carbon atom
It is an allotrope of carbon
questions.explanation: Each carbon atom in graphite is bonded to three others, leaving one free electron to move through the layers.

questions.text: What is the IUPAC name of the second member of the alkyne series?
questions.explanation: The alkyne series starts with Ethyne (2C). The second member is Propyne (3C).

questions.text: How many covalent bonds are present in a molecule of ethane (C2H6)?
questions.explanation: There are six C-H bonds and one C-C bond, totaling seven covalent bonds.

questions.text: Cyclohexane is an example of a:
questions.explanation: Cyclohexane (C6H12) consists of six carbon atoms arranged in a ring structure with single bonds.

questions.text: The heteroatom present in Chloro-methane is:
questions.explanation: Heteroatoms are atoms other than carbon and hydrogen that replace hydrogen in a hydrocarbon chain.

questions.text: Denatured alcohol is ethanol made unfit for drinking by adding:
questions.explanation: Methanol is poisonous and blue dyes are added to identify industrial alcohol and prevent misuse.

questions.text: Which of the following belongs to the same homologous series as C3H8?
questions.explanation: C3H8 is propane (alkane). C4H10 is butane (alkane), following CnH2n+2.

questions.text: Carbon cannot form C4+ cation because:
options: It would require a huge amount of energy to remove 4 electrons
questions.explanation: Removing four electrons from a carbon atom would leave a tiny cation with 6 protons holding onto only 2 electrons, which is energetically unfavorable.
